title: lophiolepis ligularis
intro: Lophiolepis ligularis, also known as the Indian cork tree or Cork Oak, is a deciduous tree native to the Himalayan region and parts of India. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lophiolepis ligularis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Lophiolepis ligularis include the bark, leaves, and roots, which can be made into decoctions, infusions, or powder for medicinal use.
